label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's go through the passage and see what we can infer. The question is asking for an inference based on the information provided. The passage talks about the history of volleyball starting in 1895 when William G. Morgan created it, inspired by basketball but for middle-aged men. He made a game called "mintonette" which used a net and ball, similar to badminton but with hands instead of rackets. First, let's look at the options: A) Volleyball develops from the game of "mintonette". Well, the passage says Morgan created "mintonette" and that volleyball's history dates back to that. So maybe A is correct. The passage says "he created the game of 'mintonette.'" And then later refers to it as volleyball history. So the original name was mintonette, which became volleyball. So A seems correct. B) Most of the competitive volleyball teams are in the college. The passage mentions high school, college, women, men, children, mixed teams, etc. But it doesn't say most are in college. So B is probably not correct. C) The rules of volleyball continue to change as they are easy to learn. The passage says the rules continued to change and adjust until it became the game today. But the reason for the changes isn't stated as because they are easy to learn. The easy to learn part is mentioned as a reason why it's popular. So C might be incorrect because the reason for changing rules isn't given as being due to ease of learning. The passage says "the rules continued to change and adjust throughout the history of volleyball, until the game became what we have today." So the changing rules are not connected to being easy to learn. So C is incorrect. D) Different rules have appeared to cut down the costs. The passage mentions that "in order to cut down the equipment costs, the players played the ball with their hands." But that was when Morgan was creating the game, not about the rules changing later. The rules that were set later (court size, number of players, etc.) don't mention cutting costs. So D might not be correct. The cost-cutting was
